# AbstractSkill

> The Agent Skills (`SKILL.md`) contract library for AbstractFramework: parse
> and validate skills, discover them on disk with progressive disclosure, hash
> them for evolution and tamper detection, compose their tool declarations with
> an operator grant, and classify their trust. Dependency-light (PyYAML only);
> executes nothing and imposes no runtime limits — hosts consume its contracts.

"Flows run; skills are activated." AbstractSkill owns the portable skill
contract so `abstractruntime`, `abstractgateway`, and thin clients share
identical semantics without duplicating parsers.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): overview, package scope, hashing contract, install.
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md): parse, discover, hash, compose, evaluate trust.
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): components, data flow, verdict precedence (Mermaid diagrams).
- [API reference](docs/api.md): all public functions and types.
- [FAQ](docs/faq.md): common questions and limitations.
- [Troubleshooting](docs/troubleshooting.md): symptoms, diagnostics, and fixes.
- [Trust model](docs/trust.md): validated skills, do-not-use advisories, guidance, fail-closed verdict.
- [Curated skills catalog](docs/skills-catalog.md): reviewed+pinned third-party skills, tiers, curated-only vendoring.
- [Skills vs workflows vs MCP](docs/skills-flows-mcp.md): which extension mechanism when — comparison table, decision rules, package responsibilities.
- [Trust-network position](docs/trust-network-position.md): join / leverage / build.
- [Security policy](SECURITY.md): what the library does and does not guarantee.
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): release history.

## Key APIs

- `parse_skill_md(text)` → `SkillDocument` (spec-validated; LF/CRLF/CR).
- `FilesystemSkillLoader(roots).discover()/.load(name)` (progressive; loud on broken skills).
- `hash_skill_tree(dir)`, `inspect_skill_dir(dir)` (tamper hash + `has_scripts`).
- `effective_tools(grant, skills)` (grant ∩ declared; never widens).
- `evaluate_trust(registry, tree_hash=..., has_scripts=...)` → `TrustVerdict` (blocked / requires_review / attachable).
- `select_skills_for_context(registry, shelf_root, names)` → `SkillSelection` (the ONE trust-gated activation pipeline; names-only calls derive sources from the registry).

## Planning

- [Backlog overview](docs/backlog/overview.md) and the skill-trust track (`docs/backlog/planned/trust/`).
